semantic_description: The fundamental brioche knit stitch. The stitch is worked together with its paired yarn over from the previous row, producing the characteristic lofty column of brioche fabric. In stitch dictionaries and chart glossaries, BRK is distinct from knit-below techniques even though the fabric can look similar.

written_forms: knit the stitch together with its paired yarn over
brk: knit the next stitch and its accompanying yo together as one
ambiguity_notes: 'bark' is an older informal abbreviation (bark = brioche knit). Nancy Marchant's standard is lowercase 'brk'. 'BRK1' is sometimes seen but 'brk' is preferred per Marchant's Knitting Fresh Brioche.
'k1b' in brioche contexts may mean brioche knit, but in ordinary stitch patterns it usually means knit 1 below. Context matters.

rs: Insert the needle through the next stitch and its paired yarn over together knitwise
Knit them together as one brioche stitch

semantic_description: The fundamental brioche purl stitch. The stitch and its paired yarn over are purled together as one unit, forming the purl-side counterpart to brioche knit. Standard brioche glossaries distinguish BRP from ordinary purl-below techniques.

written_forms: purl the stitch together with its paired yarn over
brp: purl the next stitch and its yo together
ambiguity_notes: 'burp' is the informal name but not used as a chart abbreviation. Per Nancy Marchant, 'brp' is standard.

rs: Insert the needle purlwise through the next stitch and its paired yarn over together
Purl them together as one brioche stitch

semantic_description: The setup move that creates a brioche pair: the next stitch is slipped and the working yarn is taken over it, pairing the slipped stitch with a yarn over for the next row. StitchMastery and brioche references also describe sl1yof variants as source-specific renderings of this setup family.

written_forms: bring yarn to the front, slip 1 purlwise, then bring the yarn over the needle
yf sl1yo
with yarn in front, slip 1 purlwise, bring yarn over right needle
bring yarn forward, slip 1 purlwise, take yarn over needle to back
sl1 with accompanying yo
ambiguity_notes: sl1yo models brioche positions (1 in, 1 out) not raw stitch+yo loops. The raw loop count after the move is 2 (1 slip + 1 yo on needle), but in brioche accounting the pair counts as one unit.

rs: Bring the yarn to the front
Slip the next stitch purlwise
Take the yarn over the needle so it lies paired with the slipped stitch

semantic_description: A generic left-leaning brioche decrease family. It represents the class of left-slanting brioche decreases without forcing one exact abbreviation. Exact members include BRSSK/BRKSSK on the knit side and BRSSP/BRKSSP on the purl side.

written_forms: work a left-leaning brioche decrease as specified in the pattern
ambiguity_notes: This is the generic left-slanting brioche decrease. In practice most patterns use 'brssk' or 'brsssk' specifically. The 'brLsl dec' abbreviation is used by some SM-based patterns but is not universal.

Family abbreviation used by Nancy Marchant. The knit-side form is brssk/brkssk; the purl-side form is brssp/brkssp. Resolve to the specific operation when pattern context indicates which face is being worked.

rs: Work the specific left-leaning brioche decrease named in the pattern or legend

semantic_description: A generic right-leaning brioche decrease family. It covers right-slanting brioche decreases without collapsing them into one exact abbreviation. Exact members include BRK2TOG on the knit side and BRP2TOG on the purl side.

written_forms: work a right-leaning brioche decrease as specified in the pattern
ambiguity_notes: Same situation as br_dec_left. In practice most patterns use 'brk2tog'. Generic form.
Family abbreviation used by Nancy Marchant. The knit-side form is brk2tog; the purl-side form is brp2tog. Resolve to the specific operation when pattern context indicates which face is being worked.

rs: Work the specific right-leaning brioche decrease named in the pattern or legend

semantic_description: A left-leaning single brioche decrease worked on the knit side. Many modern brioche references abbreviate this as BRSSK, while StitchMastery labels the same operation BRKSSK. It is the knit-side left counterpart to BRK2TOG.

written_forms: slip stitches as directed and brioche knit the slipped stitches together for a left-slanting decrease

rs: Slip the leading brioche stitch or pair as directed by the pattern
Work the remaining brioche stitches together to create a left-leaning decrease
Complete the BRSSK / BRKSSK decrease following the pattern's brioche convention

semantic_description: A right-leaning single brioche decrease worked on the knit side by brioche-knitting two brioche units together. This is the right-slanting knit-side counterpart to BRSSK / BRKSSK.

written_forms: brioche knit two brioche units together

rs: Insert the needle through the next two brioche units together knitwise
Brioche knit them together as one stitch

semantic_description: A left-leaning brioche 4-to-1 decrease worked on the knit side. StitchMastery names this BRKSSSK, while brioche references often shorten it to BRSSSK. It is the left-leaning counterpart to BRK3TOG.

written_forms: work a left-leaning 4-to-1 brioche decrease

rs: Work the left-leaning 4-to-1 brioche decrease method specified by the pattern

semantic_description: A right-leaning brioche 4-to-1 decrease worked on the knit side by brioche-knitting three brioche units together. It is the stronger right-slanting counterpart to BRSSSK.

written_forms: brioche knit three brioche units together

rs: Insert the needle through the required brioche units together knitwise
Brioche knit them together as one stitch

semantic_description: A left-leaning single brioche decrease worked on the purl side. StitchMastery labels this BRKSSP, while many brioche references shorten it to BRSSP. It is the purl-side left counterpart to BRP2TOG.

written_forms: work a left-leaning purl-side brioche decrease

rs: Work the left-leaning purl-side brioche decrease specified by the pattern

semantic_description: A right-leaning single brioche decrease worked on the purl side by purling two brioche units together. It is the purl-side right counterpart to BRSSP / BRKSSP.

written_forms: brioche purl two brioche units together

rs: Insert the needle purlwise through the next two brioche units together
Purl them together as one brioche stitch

semantic_description: A left-leaning brioche 4-to-1 decrease worked on the purl side. StitchMastery labels the same family BRKSSSP. It is the purl-side left counterpart to BRP3TOG.

written_forms: work a left-leaning 4-to-1 purl-side brioche decrease

rs: Work the left-leaning 4-to-1 purl-side brioche decrease specified by the pattern

semantic_description: A right-leaning brioche 4-to-1 decrease worked on the purl side by purling three brioche units together. It is the purl-side right counterpart to BRSSSP / BRKSSSP.

written_forms: brioche purl three brioche units together

rs: Insert the needle purlwise through the required brioche units together
Purl them together as one stitch

semantic_description: A centered brioche double decrease that reduces three brioche positions to one while keeping the decrease visually centered. StitchMastery lists this as a distinct brioche centred double decrease.

written_forms: work the centered brioche double decrease

rs: Work the centered brioche double decrease method specified by the pattern

semantic_description: The purl-side centered brioche double decrease. StitchMastery distinguishes it from the knit-side brioche centered double decrease, so it should not be merged into a plain centered decrease entry.

written_forms: work the centered brioche double purl decrease

rs: Work the purl-side centered brioche decrease method specified by the pattern

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-3 brioche increase worked as brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit into one position. StitchMastery lists BRKYOBRK as a distinct brioche increase symbol and brioche references use it as a standard 3-stitch increase abbreviation.

alternate_names: brioche knit yarn over brioche knit
written_forms: work brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit into one stitch

rs: Work a brioche knit into the stitch
Yarn over
Work another brioche knit into the same stitch

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-3 brioche increase worked as brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit into one stitch. StitchMastery lists this separately from BRKYOBRK, so it should not be collapsed into a generic brioche increase.

alternate_names: brioche knit brioche purl brioche knit
written_forms: work brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit into one stitch

rs: Work a brioche knit into the stitch
Work a brioche purl into the same stitch
Work a second brioche knit into the same stitch

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-5 brioche increase worked as brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit into one stitch. Brioche references also describe this as BRKYO BRKYO BRK or a 4-stitch brioche increase.

alternate_names: brioche knit yarn over brioche knit yarn over brioche knit
written_forms: work brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit, yarn over, brioche knit into one stitch

stitchmastery: brioche - brk, yo, brk, yo, brk

rs: Work a brioche knit into the stitch without dropping it
Yarn over
Work another brioche knit into the same stitch
Yarn over again
Work a third brioche knit into the same stitch

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-5 brioche increase worked as brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit into one stitch. StitchMastery lists this separately from the yarn-over-centered 1-to-5 brioche increase, so it should remain a distinct exact operation.

alternate_names: brioche knit brioche purl brioche knit brioche purl brioche knit
written_forms: work brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit, brioche purl, brioche knit into one stitch

stitchmastery: brioche - brk, brp, brk, brp, brk

rs: Work a brioche knit into the stitch
Work a brioche purl into the same stitch
Work another brioche knit into the same stitch
Work another brioche purl into the same stitch
Work a final brioche knit into the same stitch

semantic_description: A brioche knit worked through the back loop or in a twisted orientation. StitchMastery distinguishes BRK tbl from an ordinary BRK, so it should be modeled as a separate brioche variant rather than grouped under plain brioche knit.

written_forms: work the brioche knit through the back loop

rs: Insert the needle through the brioche stitch and its paired yarn over in the back-loop orientation
Knit them together through the back loop

semantic_description: A brioche purl worked through the back loop or in a twisted orientation. StitchMastery distinguishes BRP tbl from ordinary BRP, making it a separate brioche variant.

written_forms: work the brioche purl through the back loop

rs: Insert the needle through the brioche stitch and its paired yarn over in the back-loop orientation
Purl them together through the back loop

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-3 brioche increase worked on the purl side as brioche purl, yarn over, brioche purl into one stitch. The purl-side mirror of BRKYOBRK. Used in reversible brioche patterns to create symmetric increases on both faces of the fabric.

alternate_names: brioche purl yarn over brioche purl
written_forms: work brioche purl, yarn over, brioche purl into one stitch

rs: Work a brioche purl into the stitch without dropping it
Yarn over
Work another brioche purl into the same stitch

semantic_description: A centered 1-to-3 brioche increase worked on the purl side as brioche purl, brioche knit, brioche purl into one stitch. The purl-side mirror of BRK/BRP/BRK. Used in reversible brioche for symmetric increase pairs across both faces.

alternate_names: brioche purl brioche knit brioche purl
written_forms: work brioche purl, brioche knit, brioche purl into one stitch

rs: Work a brioche purl into the stitch
Work a brioche knit into the same stitch
Work a second brioche purl into the same stitch
